message:
test(codec): metamorphic robustness fuzz — decode never panics on any byte sequence

Adds frankenterm/codec/tests/proptest_codec_decode_fuzz.rs. The
metamorphic relation under test is the single invariant mux code
depends on: for *any* byte sequence a hostile client could send over
an unauthenticated mux socket, `Pdu::decode` / `Pdu::stream_decode`
must terminate with either `Ok(DecodedPdu)` or a typed
`anyhow::Error`. Never a panic, never an infinite loop. A panic on
the decode path is a cheap denial-of-service against the mux server
— a client can kill the server just by sending bytes.

Three properties, 2048 proptest cases each (~6k fuzz iterations):

  * `decode_random_bytes_never_panics` — pure-random Vec<u8> of length
    0..=2048 fed directly into `Pdu::decode`. Exercises the leb128
    length / ident / serial header edges and the shallow-header
    early-exit paths.

  * `decode_structured_header_never_panics` — hand-rolls a plausible
    leb128 header (length, ident, serial) with the ident drawn from
    the registered-variant ident range (0..=75) plus a few unknown-
    ident samples (u64::MAX / 2, u64::MAX, 1_000_000) and appends
    random bytes as the body. Half the time the length word's
    top-bit compression flag is set, forcing the decoder onto the
    zstd branch even when the body is not a valid compressed blob.
    This exercises the per-variant varbincode deserialize path —
    where most of the real codec logic lives and where a debug_
    assert panic in a nested serde impl would otherwise hide.

  * `stream_decode_random_bytes_never_panics` — same random-byte
    surface but through `Pdu::stream_decode`, which the mux server
    calls on every socket read. Extra invariants asserted:
    - `Ok(Some(_))` means the buffer was advanced (frame consumed);
    - `Ok(None)` means partial frame and the buffer was unchanged;
    - `Err(_)` is allowed but must not corrupt the buffer state
      (checked implicitly — a panic would abort the property).

Each successful `Ok(decoded)` is sanity-checked via
`assert_decoded_pdu_is_well_formed`: the decoded serial is accepted
as any u64, and `pdu_name()` must be non-empty (the `Invalid`
fall-through variant for unknown idents counts as a legitimate name).

Verified: `cargo test --manifest-path frankenterm/codec/Cargo.toml
--test proptest_codec_decode_fuzz` — `test result: ok. 3 passed;
0 failed` in 2.83s at /tmp/ft-pane6-target after a 1m 44s cold
compile.

Harness ran clean on first pass — no regressions surfaced on any of
the 6,144 fuzz inputs, which tells us the current decode path is
robust against the edges this harness explores. Saves the proptest
regression seeds in the gitignored `.proptest-regressions` file so
any future regression is re-tried first.
